name: Corned Beef Brisket W/Vegetables & Horseradis
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour water into the pressure cooker.
2. Add corned beef brisket to the pot.
3. Over high heat, bring water to a rolling boil. Skim any residue from the surface.
4. Add garlic and bay leaves.
5. Secure the lid over the pressure cooker.
6. Over high heat, develop steam until high pressure is reached.
7. Reduce heat to maintain pressure and cook for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
8. Release pressure according to manufacturer's directions and remove the lid.
9. Add vegetables (carrots, cabbage, potatoes, and turnips) to the brisket and liquid in the pressure cooker.
10. Stir gently and secure the lid.
11. Over high heat, develop steam until high pressure is reached again.
12. Reduce heat to maintain pressure and cook for 6 minutes.
13. Release pressure according to manufacturer's directions and remove the lid.
14. Transfer vegetables to a platter.
15. Slice the brisket across the grain and arrange slices on the platter.
16. Serve with Horseradish Sauce.
Horseradish Sauce:
- Prepared horseradish (1/3 cup)
- Prepared mustard (1 teaspoon)
- Sour cream (1/4 cup)
- Lemon juice (1 tablespoon)
- Garlic (2 cloves), crushed
- Green onions (6 green parts, minced)
- Sugar (1/8 teaspoon)
- Salt (1/8 teaspoon)
- White pepper (1/8 teaspoon)
Combine horseradish, mustard, sour cream, and lemon juice in a bowl. Blend thoroughly. Add garlic, green onions, sugar, salt, and white pepper; mix well. Serve with corned beef, roast beef, or roast pork.
